flashcards.definition: TRH → TSH → T3/T4; negative feedback from thyroid hormones.
CRH → ACTH → cortisol; stress axis; negative feedback from cortisol.
GnRH → FSH/LH → sex hormones; reproductive regulation.
A hormone that stimulates another endocrine gland (e.g., TSH, ACTH, FSH, LH).
One hormone enhances the effect of another (e.g., thyroid hormones potentiate catecholamine effects).
